methods.name: kobold.tests.compare.test_list.TestMultiMatch.test_unordered_basic
documentation: Unordered

classes.name: kobold.NotPresent
documentation:
Used to explicitly specify that something isn't present,
or wasn't supplied

functions.name: kobold.doubles.get_stub_class
documentation:
Creates a class whose methods are all spies.

methods_to_add is dictionary or a list of methods to 
add to the class that is returned.  The methods will
all be SpyFunctions.  If methods_to_add is a dictionary, the
method names are the keys, and the return types are the values.
If methods_to_add is a list, the return types are all None.

is_callable is a flag that defaults to False.  If it is 
set to True, we will add a __call__ method to the class
that will make instances of the class behave like a SpyFunctions.

functions.name: kobold.doubles.dictify_routes
documentation:
Used to recurse through the routes dictionary to return nested
dictionaries (rather than instances of routes and conditions)
for pprint display purposes

classes.name: kobold.doubles.StubFunction

methods.name: kobold.doubles.StubFunction.returns
documentation: This will make StubFunction return this value when called

methods.name: kobold.doubles.StubFunction.raises
documentation: This will make StubFunction raise this exception when called

methods.name: kobold.doubles.StubFunction.calls
documentation:
This will make StubFunction call this function when called
(and return the value that the function returns)

A Stub is a Test Double that is intended to replace a real function
with one that just behaves as configured.  Usually, this means
returning a predefined value.  We extend this to mean returning a
value, raising an exception, or calling another function and returning
the result.

The constructor takes three optional params: returns, raises and calls.

-If returns is supplied, the function will return the value of returns
 when called.

-If raises is supplied, the function will raise the value of raises
 when called

-If calls is supplied, the function will call "calls" when it is 
 called.  We will return the value that the function returns.

classes.name: kobold.doubles.SpyFunction

methods.name: kobold.doubles.SpyFunction.reset
documentation: Forget any calls this spy has recorded

A Spy is a Test Double that makes a record of each time it is called,
typically to be queried later.  SpyFunction saves the args and
kwargs everytime it's called.  It then defers to whatever stub function
factory is supplied (by default StubFunction).  The args and kwargs
are passed into StubFunction, so that a Spy can specify what it's
return behavior is

classes.name: kobold.doubles.StubRoutingException
documentation:
This exception indicates that a RoutableStubFunction could
not determine which route to follow.  A RoutableStubFunction
should always be able to identify exactly one route to 
follow for each function call

classes.name: kobold.compare.hints.ParsingHint
documentation:
Tells the comparison function to parse the 
second argument ("actual") in a specific way to
get a simple data-structure

functions.name: kobold.assertions.assert_response_matches
documentation:
If the responses don't match (in the kobold.compare sense),
raise an AssertionError

functions.name: kobold.assertions.assert_equal
documentation:
If two data structures don't match (in the kobold.compare sense),
raise an AssertionError

functions.name: kobold.response.response_matches
documentation:
Compare two HTTP responses (using kobold.compare).
A type_compare may be provided, but if none is set,
the default is for hashes to be compared using "full" mode,
and for lists to be compared using "ordered" mode.
Headers, however, are supplied an override to ensure that 
they are compared in "existing" mode.

The first two arguments are the expected and actual response.
Responses are expected to be a hash, with three keys: status_code,
body and headers.  By default, the status_code of the response is 
expected to be 200 (ie. success of the request is enforced), and no 
headers are enforced.  

Body will be a string, unless the actual response has a 
Content-Type header of "application/json".  In that case,
we parse the JSON and set body to be the parsed data structure.
Remember, by default all keys in the body will be compared.
type_compare can be set to "existing" to change this behavior.

Ultimately, the expected and response hashes are compared using
kobold.compare, and the result is returned.  In the case of a match,
the result will be the string "match".  In the case of a mismatch,
the result will be a tuple of two elements - the first describing
the mismatched values in the first argument, and the second
describing the mismatched values in the second argument.

classes.name: kobold.swap.SafeSwap

methods.name: kobold.swap.SafeSwap.swap
documentation:
Given a host object, a member name, and some other object,
replace the member of that name on that host with the given object.
This can be undone with rollback()

methods.name: kobold.swap.SafeSwap.install_proxy
documentation:
Replace a member with a function that does something else,
and then calls that original member (this is very similar
to the idea of dynamically decorating a function at runtime, 
except that the decoration will be undone when rollback is called.

Typically, the idea here is to inject a spy in front of the
original functionality.  So, the function will have the same
result as it would without this replacement, except the spy remembers
each call.

methods.name: kobold.swap.SafeSwap.unswap
documentation: Rollback a specific replacement

methods.name: kobold.swap.SafeSwap.rollback
documentation: Rollback all replacements (at the end of a test, for instance)

Instances of this class manage runtime replacements of members,
and enable all replacements to be undone with the rollback method

functions.name: kobold.hash_functions.project
documentation:
Given a dict and a list of keys, return a
dict that only includes the keys in the list

functions.name: kobold.hash_functions.merge
documentation:
Mutate a dict (to_mutate) with defaults from the first.
Obviously, if to_mutate already has a key, don't override it.
Return the mutated dict.

functions.name: kobold.hash_functions.combine
documentation:
Take two dicts and create a third dict with all the keys
from both.  If both dicts share a key, the second dict
overrides the first.  Return the new dict.

classes.name: kobold.hash_functions.HashableDict
documentation: Based on https://stackoverflow.com/a/1151686/2619588'''

functions.name: kobold.compare.compare
documentation: A wrapper around Compare.compare

classes.name: kobold.compare.DontCare
documentation:
Used as the "expected" argument in a comparison to mean "I don't 
care what the 'actual' object is, as long as some rules hold."  
By default, the rule is not_none_or_missing.

Rules:

not_none_or_missing: The second argument can be anything,
as long as it is neither None nor kobold.NotPresent.

list: The second argument can be anything, as long as it is
an instance of list.

json: The second argument may be any json-parseable string

iso8601_datetime: The second argument must be able to be 
evaluated as an ISO 8601 datetime string (eg. 
2017-01-01T00:00:00)

no_rules: The second argument may be anything - I literally
do not care.

classes.name: kobold.compare.StructuredString
documentation: An attempt to provide recursive comparison for strings

methods.name: kobold.compare.Compare.display
documentation: These "display" functions are used by the unordered list comparison
for intelligently displaying unordered diffs of lists
